sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Great Elm Capital Corp. ('GECC”) Schedules First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
feed.summary: Great Elm Capital Corp. (GECC) has announced it will release its first-quarter financial results for the period ending March 31, 2026, after market close on Monday, May 4, 2026. A conference call to discuss these results will be held on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET. Investors can access the call via dial-in numbers or a webcast available on the company's website.

feed.title: Farmland Shareholders Reaffirm Governance, Executive Pay Practices
feed.summary: Farmland Partners Inc. shareholders have reaffirmed the company's governance and executive pay practices during their annual meeting on April 28, 2026. They re-elected all five director nominees, ratified Crowe LLP as the independent auditor, and approved executive compensation on an advisory basis, opting for annual advisory votes. The company's stock, FPI, currently holds a "Hold" rating with a $12.50 price target.

feed.title: FSEP ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns BOATS:FSEP
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the FT Vest U.S. Equity Buffer ETF - September (FSEP), focusing on its dividends, returns, and operational details. FSEP uses options on the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) to buffer against the first 10% of losses over a one-year period, in exchange for foregoing some upside and SPY's dividends. Key information includes its $1.25 billion AUM, a 0.85% expense ratio, and its active management style, with the ETF not paying dividends directly to its holders.

feed.title: Investors Buy the Dip: Fidelity’s FSOL ETF Sees Big Inflow Despite Solana Slump
authors: TipRanks FX Crypto ETFs Auto-Generated Newsdesk
feed.summary: Despite a significant slump in Solana's price, Fidelity's Solana Fund (FSOL) ETF saw a substantial inflow of $7.79 million on April 30, 2026. This influx, representing 7.48% of the fund's assets under management, suggests that investors are viewing Solana's weakness as a buying opportunity. The move highlights the common investment strategy in digital assets of capitalizing on volatility, although it also increases potential downside risk if Solana's price continues to fall.

feed.title: Nashville mayor considers cash incentives for Starbucks in rare move
feed.summary: Nashville Mayor Freddie O'Connell is reportedly considering offering cash incentives to Starbucks for its corporate expansion in Tennessee, a move that would represent a significant policy shift for his administration. O'Connell has previously avoided such cash-per-job incentives in an effort to attract high-paying jobs without negatively impacting local businesses. This potential deal highlights Nashville's ongoing effort to balance economic development with local business concerns.

feed.title: Tesla Semi production ramps up with first vehicle off high-volume line
feed.summary: Tesla has announced that its first Semi truck has rolled off the high-volume production line at Gigafactory Nevada, marking a significant milestone after its initial unveiling in 2017. The electric truck, boasting a 500-mile range model costing $300,000 and a 300-mile range version at $260,000, is now entering operational pilots with companies like MDB Transportation for last-mile container moves. Additionally, infrastructure for the Semi is expanding, with Pilot Travel Centers partnering to install Megachargers at truck stops across major interstates, and Tesla actively recruiting talent to support commercial operations and manufacturing in Texas.

feed.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From HCA Healthcare’s Q1 Earnings Call
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare's Q1 2026 results met revenue and profit expectations but were impacted by a milder respiratory season and a severe winter storm, leading to a negative market reaction. Analyst questions focused on EBITDA shortfalls, Medicaid program timing, payer behavior shifts, network expansion, and bad debt accruals. Despite these challenges, management highlighted mitigating factors like Medicaid benefits and cost efficiencies.

feed.title: The 5 Most Interesting Analyst Questions From HCA Healthcare’s Q1 Earnings Call
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare's Q1 2026 results met revenue and profit expectations, but market reaction was negative due to lower patient volumes from a mild respiratory season and a winter storm. Management discussions highlighted how cost efficiencies and Medicaid benefits largely offset these volume shortfalls. Analysts focused on issues such as EBITDA drivers, Medicaid program approvals, changing payer behavior, network expansion, and bad debt accruals.
